locus_id: rs12913832 (chr15 HERC2 enhancer regulating OCA2) — stratification anchor
population: European ancestry; CanPath biobank, Canada (discovery N=5,48
evidence_quote: rs12913832 itself showed a dosage effect (not fully dominant) and was strongly associated with central heterochromia (ΔE, p = 2.97×10⁻⁷): one G copy increases the likelihood of different inner/outer iris colours — potentially explaining many self-reported discordances (people wit
classification_notes: HERC2 is itself on the canonical effector list; paper states HERC2 enhancer regulates OCA2 (also canonical). Textbook eye-colour mechanism, not in scope for effector-uncertain set.
author_explanation_quote: rs12913832 alone... having one derived allele (G) increases the likelihood of lighter eyes... marker does not behave in an entirely dominant fashion

locus_id: chr5 ~33.95Mb SLC45A2 credible set (GG background) — rep rs35389; 4-SNP cluster (rs35389, rs35395, rs16891982, rs35407)
rsid: rs35389 (representative of 4-SNP FINEMAP credible set incl. coding non-syn rs16891982)
pvalue: P(FE)=1.18E-12; FINEMAP PIP=0.408 (top of credible set)
evidence_quote: SLC45A2 region (rs35389 top PIP 0.408, in LD with rs16891982)
author_explanation_quote: rs35389 emerged as the most likely causal variant...in linkage disequilibrium [with rs16891982, functional SLC45A2 transporter variant]
source_location: Abbatangelo2026_SciRep_eyecolour_discordance.pdf p.12 (Eye colour darkening in GG stratified individuals: SLC45A2)

locus_id: chr9 ~12.69Mb TYRP1 (GG background) — rep rs2733836; Meyer2020 candidates rs62538956 (genome-wide sig here) and rs35866166 (nominal) map to same signal
rsid: rs2733836 (meta-lead/COJO-independent GG signal); Meyer candidate rs62538956 also genome-wide significant here (P=5.01E-09); rs35866166 nominal
pvalue: P(FE)=1.37E-13; FINEMAP PIP=0.068; Meyer candidate rs62538956 P=5.01E-09 (fulltext quotes 5.8E-09)
evidence_quote: Meta-lead & COJO independent signal chr9 (GG). k=2 credible sets. eQTL brain. Not previously assoc w/ eye colour. LD w/ rs1408799,rs683 (D'>0.7)
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; only the specific variant's consequence is unknown to authors -- this is the PI's first-project category (variant-mechanism gap on a textbook effector), not effector-uncertain.
author_explanation_quote: Not much is known of the variant's consequence apart from the fact that it is located 2 kilobases upstream of TYRP1

locus_id: rs1126809 (chr11 TYR missense exon 4, GG background)
pvalue: P(FE)=4.58E-13; FINEMAP PIP=0.96; replication L* P=0.00187, b* P=5.95E-05
population: European ancestry; CanPath (GG stratum, N=2,757) + replicati
evidence_quote: TYR rs1126809 (missense, exon 4; PIP 0.96 — likely causal; G allele opposes reduced OCA2 expression)
author_explanation_quote: the alternative allele (A) induces a missense mutation that renders the tyrosinase enzyme thermosensitive and less efficient

locus_id: chr14 ~92.77Mb SLC24A4 credible set (GG background) — rep rs12896399; 8-SNP cluster (rs12896399, rs12896471, rs56137766, rs35983729, rs4144266, rs4904866, rs746586, rs12883151)
rsid: rs12896399 (meta-lead/COJO-independent; IrisPlex panel SNP, used here as GWAS hit not merely for prediction); 8-SNP FINEMAP credible set. Meyer2020 candidate rs1289469 was absent from this dataset.
pvalue: P(FE)=2.05E-35 (strongest GG signal); FINEMAP PIP=0.154; replication b* P=5.61E-05
population: European ancestry; CanPath (GG stratum, N=2,757) + replicati
evidence_quote: SLC24A4 rs12896399 (blue-associated T allele, an IrisPlex SNP; G allele → higher SLC24A4 expression, compensating for low OCA2)
author_explanation_quote: a G allele at rs12896399 is associated with increased SLC24A4 expression, suggesting...compensate for the reduced expression of OCA2

locus_id: chr17 ~79.55-79.61Mb TSPAN10/NPLOC4 credible-set region (GG background) — rep rs62075722; members rs6420484 (TSPAN10 coding), rs34635363 (NPLOC4 intronic passenger)
rsid: rs62075722 (TSPAN10, meta-lead/top-PIP); credible-set members rs6420484 (TSPAN10 coding non-syn, abstract-highlighted) and rs34635363 (NPLOC4 intronic — same region, NOT a separate pigmentation gene)
nearest_gene_label: TSPAN10 (NPLOC4 is a passenger/member gene in the same credible-set region)
pvalue: P(FE)=6.36E-10; FINEMAP PIP=0.174; replication b* P=3.05E-04
population: European ancestry; CanPath (GG stratum, N=2,757) + replicati
evidence_quote: TSPAN10 rs62075722 (eQTL; G allele → higher expression, darker iris)
classification_notes: TSPAN10 is NOT on the canonical list; GTEx eQTL evidence cited as the mechanism (mechanism_proposed via eQTL). mygene.info summary shows no explicit pigmentation function for TSPAN10, though Morgan2018 independently reports LD with a TSPAN10 missense variant Y177C and cites prior murine Tspan10 knockdown reducing melanocyte migration (idx79) -- flag for cross-paper corroboration. NPLOC4 in the same region is explicitly flagged by the paper as a passenger, not an independent candidate.
author_explanation_quote: rs62075722 illustrated the highest PIP...is an eQTL in GTEx...a G allele at this locus is associated with increased expression and a darker iris colour

locus_id: rs12203592 (chr6 IRF4 intron 4 enhancer, AA+AG background)
pvalue: P(FE)=3.13E-26; FINEMAP PIP=1.00; replication L*/a*/b*/ΔE all Bonferroni-significant
population: European ancestry; CanPath (AA+AG stratum, N=2,724) + replic
evidence_quote: IRF4 rs12203592 (PIP 1.00; T allele lowers IRF4→TYR expression, lighter eyes — can override the brown genotype)
author_explanation_quote: The T allele at rs12203592 leads to decreased IRF4 expression...carriers of the T allele tend to exhibit lighter eye colour

rsid: rs7853779 (meta-lead/COJO-independent AA+AG signal); 6-SNP FINEMAP credible set
evidence_quote: variants in IRF4, OCA2 and TYRP1, which may influence eye colour presentation in individuals with the rs12913832:AA + AG genotype
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; variant-mechanism gap only (paper: 'more difficult to interpret...no other studies...investigated the role of TYRP1 SNPs').
author_explanation_quote: The observation of TYRP1 variants...in the AA+AG genetic background is both interesting, and more difficult to interpret as there are no other studies...which have investigated the role of TYRP1 SNPs in individuals with an rs12913832:AA+AG genetic background

locus_id: rs1800407 (chr15 OCA2 missense, AA+AG background, 1st credible set)
pvalue: P(FE)=4.10E-15; FINEMAP PIP=0.997; replication L* P=0.000241, a* P=0.006905
population: European ancestry; CanPath (AA+AG stratum, N=2,724) + replic
evidence_quote: OCA2 rs1800407 (missense; PIP 0.997; T allele → more green/hazel)
author_explanation_quote: The rs1800407:T allele encodes the amino acid glutamate and having just one copy of the T allele is associated with an increased probability of having green and hazel eye colours

locus_id: chr15 ~28.34Mb OCA2 blue-eye haplotype (AA+AG background, 2nd credible set) — rep rs4778138; members rs7164220 (higher PIP), rs7495174 (nominal, Duffy2007 3-SNP haplotype)
rsid: rs4778138 (COJO-independent 2nd-credible-set signal, replicated); members rs7164220 (PIP 0.472) and rs7495174 (nominal only, PIP 0.004, P=1.87E-07)
population: European ancestry; CanPath (AA+AG stratum, N=2,724) + replic
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; blue-eye haplotype tag SNPs.
author_explanation_quote: rs4778138 has been previously identified as being involved in a three-SNP haplotype system...associated with blue eyes
notes: Mechanism inferred via the general OCA2/HERC2 enhancer-expression paragraph preceding this passage; specific causal SNP between rs4778138/rs7164220 not resolved by authors ('difficult to parse which variant is the causal variant').

locus_id: rs79380392 (chr15 HERC2 intronic, AA+AG background, 3rd credible set — sub-genome-wide)
evidence_quote: NOTE: did NOT reach genome-wide significance (P=5.35E-6) - interpret with caution. CADD 5.332
classification_notes: HERC2 canonical; sub-genome-wide intronic signal, variant mechanism gap.
author_explanation_quote: rs79380392 appears to be the causal SNP in the third credible set as the only SNP with a log10BF > 2...did not reach genome-wide significance...must be interpreted with caution
notes: Statistical fine-mapping prioritization only (FINEMAP log10BF); no functional/mechanistic annotation given for this SNP.

locus_id: OCA2 — Salvo2023 AA+AG→blue candidate retests (rep rs72714116; also rs78544415 ns, rs74409036 absent)
rsid: rs72714116 (P=1.45E-07, below genome-wide); rs78544415 (P=7.47E-02, ns); rs74409036 (absent — not imputed / failed MAF QC)
coord_build: not_reported (coordinates not tabulated in this paper)
pvalue: rs72714116 P=1.45E-07 (not genome-wide sig); rs78544415 P=7.47E-02 (ns)
evidence_quote: Of Salvo et al. 2023's AA/AG→blue candidates, three were absent from this dataset (low frequency) and two were not genome-wide significant (though TYRP1/OCA2 rs72714116 reached P = 1.45×10⁻⁷).
author_explanation_quote: The other two were not genome-wide significant in our analysis (although OCA2 rs72714116 had a p-value = 1.45E-7)
